subject: GeneralKnowledge
title: GeneralKnowledge Test - 23
description: A mix of fun and educational questions covering animals, geography, science, health, and technology for Grade 4 students.

questions.q: Which of these animals is known for having a long neck and spots?
questions.explanation: Giraffes have exceptionally long necks to reach leaves high in trees and distinct spot patterns.
questions.hint: It is the tallest land animal.

questions.q: What is the capital city of Canada?
questions.explanation: Ottawa is the capital city of Canada and is located in the province of Ontario.

questions.q: Which part of a plant is usually found above ground and supports leaves and flowers?
questions.explanation: The stem supports the plant's leaves, flowers, and fruits, and transports water and nutrients from the roots.
questions.hint: It is like the 'stalk' of the plant.

questions.q: What do we call an animal that eats only meat?
questions.explanation: A carnivore is an animal that feeds primarily on the flesh of other animals.

questions.q: Which of these is a healthy snack choice?
questions.explanation: Carrots are vegetables that provide fiber and vitamins, making them a much healthier snack than sugary or processed options.

questions.q: Which community helper helps to teach students in a school?
questions.explanation: Teachers are professionals who help students learn new concepts, skills, and subjects in an educational setting.

questions.q: What is the name of our home galaxy?
questions.explanation: The Milky Way is the galaxy that contains our Solar System, including Earth.
questions.hint: It has a name like a popular candy bar.

questions.q: Which of these is a form of energy that we get from the wind?
questions.explanation: Wind energy is created by using wind turbines to harness the power of moving air.
questions.hint: It is named after the air movement itself.

questions.q: What is the freezing point of water in degrees Fahrenheit?
questions.explanation: At sea level, water freezes at 32 degrees Fahrenheit.

questions.q: Which season is known for flowers blooming and warming temperatures?
questions.explanation: Spring is the season that transitions from winter to summer, bringing new plant growth and warmer weather.
